points.text: Outlook Therapeutics resubmitted Biologics License Application (BLA) to FDA for ONS-5010/LYTENAVA (bevacizumab-vikg), a biosimilar monoclonal antibody targeting VEGF for wet age-related macular degeneration.
Invivyd announced completion of enrollment in the upsized Phase 3 DECLARATION trial for VYD2311, an investigational monoclonal antibody to prevent COVID-19.

Jade Biosciences reported positive interim Phase 1 results for JADE101, a fully human anti-APRIL monoclonal antibody, demonstrating a potential best-in-class profile with every-12-week dosing in IgA nephropathy.
Invivyd received FDA emergency use authorization in March 2024 for a monoclonal antibody in its pipeline to protect against serious viral infectious diseases.

Jade Biosciences announced positive interim Phase 1 results for JADE101, a novel anti-APRIL monoclonal antibody, showing ~70% IgA reductions sustained at 12 weeks in healthy volunteers with a projected every-12-week dosing schedule for IgA Nephropathy.

Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals — Vunakizumab — Radiographic axial spondyloarthritis — Novel humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody targeting IL-17A demonstrated superiority over placebo in ASAS20 response at 16 weeks in active disease; safety profile showed no difference in adverse events vs placebo.

Jasper Therapeutics — briquilimab — Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria (CSU) — Phase 2b protocol refiled with FDA; 67% of patients (n=6) on 240mg/180mg regimen achieved complete response at 12 weeks in BEACON study.

TG Therapeutics — BRIUMVI (ublituximab-xiiy) — Relapsing Forms of Multiple Sclerosis (RMS) — Post-hoc data published in Frontiers in Immunology showing 56.7% reduction in annualized relapse rate vs teriflunomide in treatment-naïve patients.
Alemtuzumab — Thyroid Eye Disease — Monoclonal antibody identified as a causative agent for thyroid eye disease alongside immune checkpoint inhibitors in clinical observations.
WHO experts recommended the use of an antiviral and two monoclonal antibodies in the response to the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak.

Janssen's teclistamab-cqyv (Tecvayli), a bispecific monoclonal antibody, demonstrated a 71% reduction in risk of progression or death (HR 0.29) vs investigator's choice in Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM).

Prophylactic tocilizumab (Actemra) enabled outpatient administration of bispecific monoclonal antibodies teclistamab and talquetamab-tgvs by reducing cytokine release syndrome (CRS) to 12.2% with no grade 3+ events in the Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trial.

Teclistamab monotherapy achieved an 84.5% overall response rate (ORR) and 65.9% complete response (CR) or better rate in RRMM patients with 1-3 prior lines of therapy, significantly outperforming standard care (PVd/Kd).

Bristol Myers Squibb — Mezigdomide (CC-92480) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM) — Phase 3 SUCCESSOR-2 trial met primary endpoint: 52% reduction in risk of progression or death vs carfilzomib/dexamethasone (HR 0.48; p<0.0001) in patients previously exposed to anti-CD38 monoclonal antibodies.

Bristol Myers Squibb — Mezigdomide (CC-92480) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM) — Phase 3 SUCCESSOR-2 trial demonstrated median progression-free survival of 18.0 months vs 8.3 months in anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody-exposed patients.
Outlook Therapeutics — ONS-5010/LYTENAVA (bevacizumab-vikg) — Wet Age-Related Macular Degeneration — Resubmitted BLA to FDA; biosimilar monoclonal antibody targeting VEGF-A.
Invivyd — VYD2311 — COVID-19 prevention — Phase 3 DECLARATION trial enrollment completed; pivotal randomized, placebo-controlled study evaluating safety and efficacy at three months.

Jade Biosciences — JADE101 — IgA Nephropathy — Interim Phase 1 healthy volunteer results confirm potential for every-12-week dosing schedule and best-in-class differentiation as an anti-APRIL monoclonal antibody.

Jade Biosciences — JADE101 — IgA Nephropathy — Interim Phase 1 healthy volunteer results: ~70% IgA reduction sustained at 12 weeks after single dose; Phase 2 trial underway with interim data expected 2027; Phase 3 registrational trial initiation planned H1 2027.

Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals — Vunakizumab — Radiographic axial spondyloarthritis — Phase 2 to 3 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial met primary endpoint with significant ASAS20 response at 16 weeks; drug is a humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody selective for IL-17A.

Jasper Therapeutics — briquilimab — Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria — Phase 2b protocol updated and refiled with FDA; 75% of CSU participants (n=36) in open-label extension achieved complete response or well-controlled disease at 12 weeks with 180mg every 8 weeks.

TG Therapeutics — BRIUMVI (ublituximab-xiiy) — Relapsing Forms of Multiple Sclerosis — Phase 3 ULTIMATE I & II post-hoc analysis published; demonstrated significant improvements in disability and MRI outcomes in treatment-naïve patients.
WHO — Bundibugyo Ebola response — Ebola Virus Disease — Recommended inclusion of two monoclonal antibodies alongside an antiviral in outbreak containment strategy.

Janssen — Teclistamab-cqyv (Tecvayli) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9: HR 0.29 for PFS (71% risk reduction), 18-month PFS 69.8% vs 26.9%; HR 0.60 for OS; ORR 84.5% vs 54.2%.

SCRI / Janssen — Teclistamab + Talquetamab-tgvs — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal: Prophylactic tocilizumab reduced CRS to 12.2% (no grade 3+ events), enabling outpatient dosing; ORR 68.9% (teclistamab) and 71.4% (talquetamab).

Bristol Myers Squibb — Mezigdomide (CC-92480) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 SUCCESSOR-2 trial: MeziKd regimen (mezigdomide + carfilzomib + dexamethasone) achieved 80.2% overall response rate vs 53.4% for control in patients with prior anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody exposure.
Outlook Therapeutics — Commercialization of biosimilar monoclonal antibody — ONS-5010/LYTENAVA (bevacizumab-vikg) — BLA resubmitted to FDA; secured $5.0M registered direct offering from GMS Ventures.
Invivyd — Monoclonal antibody platform commercialization — VYD2311 (COVID-19) + PEMGARDA (pemivibart) — Phase 3 DECLARATION trial fully enrolled; Q1 2026 PEMGARDA revenue $13.7M (+22% YoY).

Jade Biosciences — Anti-APRIL monoclonal antibody development — JADE101 — Phase 1 healthy volunteer study completed; engineered for half-life extension with ~27-day serum half-life to support 12-week dosing.

Jade Biosciences — Development of best-in-class anti-APRIL monoclonal antibody — JADE101 — Clinical-stage biotech advancing JADE101 for IgA Nephropathy; Phase 1 data supports potential for superior durability and dosing interval vs first-generation agents.

Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals — Monoclonal antibody expansion in rheumatology — Vunakizumab (IL-17A mAb) — Phase 2/3 data supports efficacy in radiographic axial spondyloarthritis; previously approved for moderate to severe plaque psoriasis in China.
Jasper Therapeutics — Clinical-stage antibody therapy development — briquilimab (anti-c-Kit/CD117) — Q1 2026 cash balance $14.1M; net loss $1.2M; advancing briquilimab in CSU and asthma indications.
TG Therapeutics — Commercial-stage B-cell disease treatments — BRIUMVI (ublituximab-xiiy) — FDA-approved for RMS; post-hoc data from Phase 3 ULTIMATE I & II trials published in June 2026.

Janssen — Bispecific monoclonal antibody commercialization in myeloma — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) + Talquetamab (Talvey) — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 met primary PFS and secondary OS endpoints; Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal supports outpatient administration strategy.

Bristol Myers Squibb — Advancing oral immunomodulatory therapy in myeloma — Mezigdomide (CC-92480) — Phase 3 SUCCESSOR-2 data presented at ASCO 2026 showing superior efficacy in anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody-pretreated RRMM population.

LYTENAVA (bevacizumab-vikg) — Mechanism: VEGF-A inhibition via monoclonal antibody — Phase: BLA resubmission (previously Phase 3) — Population: Wet age-related macular degeneration — Comparator: Lucentis (ranibizumab) — Status: Regulatory review pending.

VYD2311 — Mechanism: Investigational monoclonal antibody — Phase: 3 — Population: Adults for COVID-19 prevention — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoint: Symptomatic COVID-19 incidence at 3 months; enrollment completed.

JADE101 — Mechanism: Fully human monoclonal antibody selectively blocking APRIL with ultra-high binding affinity and half-life extension — Phase: 1 — Population: Healthy volunteers (Phase 1) / IgA Nephropathy (target) — Results: Potent sustained IgA suppression in non-human primates; serum half-life ~27 days; supports 12-week dosing.

JADE101 — Mechanism: Anti-APRIL monoclonal antibody with ultra-high binding affinity to block pathogenic IgA production — Phase: 1 (Interim) — Population: Healthy volunteers — Comparator: Not specified in interim data — Endpoints/Results: ~70% IgA reduction sustained at 12 weeks; faster, deeper, and more durable effect than first-generation anti-APRIL or dual APRIL/BAFF agents; well-tolerated across all doses.

Vunakizumab — Mechanism: Humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody selectively targeting IL-17A — Phase: 2 to 3 — Population: Active radiographic axial spondyloarthritis — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoints/Results: Superior ASAS20 response at 16 weeks; no difference in adverse events vs placebo.

briquilimab — Mechanism: Anti-c-Kit (CD117) antibody targeting mast cells — Phase: 2b — Population: Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria — Comparator: None (dose selection study) — Results: 67% complete response at 12 weeks with 240mg/180mg regimen; low frequency of KIT-related AEs.

ublituximab-xiiy (BRIUMVI) — Mechanism: Anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody — Phase: 3 (ULTIMATE I & II) — Population: Treatment-naïve Relapsing Forms of MS — Comparator: Teriflunomide — Results: 56.7% reduction in annualized relapse rate; significant disability and MRI improvements.

Alemtuzumab — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody (CD52) — Clinical Observation: Associated with induction of thyroid eye disease in patients receiving treatment; distinct from immune checkpoint inhibitor-induced cases.

Two monoclonal antibodies — Mechanism: Not specified in source — Phase: Not specified — Population: Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak response — Comparator: Not specified — Endpoints/Results: Recommended by WHO experts for outbreak containment.

Teclistamab-cqyv — Mechanism: Bispecific T-cell engager (CD3 x BCMA) monoclonal antibody — Phase: 3 (MajesTEC-9) — Population: RRMM (1-3 prior lines) — Comparator: PVd or Kd — Endpoints: PFS HR 0.29, OS HR 0.60, ORR 84.5%, CR+ 65.9%.

Talquetamab-tgvs — Mechanism: Bispecific T-cell engager (CD3 x GPRC5D) monoclonal antibody — Phase: 2 (OPTec/OPTal) — Population: RRMM — Comparator: N/A (single arm with prophylaxis) — Endpoints: ORR 71.4%, CRS rate 28.5% (all grade 1-2) with tocilizumab prophylaxis.

Teclistamab-cqyv (Tecvayli) — Mechanism: Bispecific BCMAxCD3 antibody — Phase: 2 (OPTec arm A) — Population: RRMM — Comparator: None (single arm with tocilizumab) — Endpoints: ORR 68.9%, 8.9% CRS events (all grade 1) with tocilizumab prophylaxis.

Mezigdomide (CC-92480) — Mechanism: Cereblon E3 ligase modulator — Phase: 3 (SUCCESSOR-2) — Population: Anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody- and lenalidomide-exposed RRMM — Comparator: Carfilzomib + dexamethasone — Endpoints: 52% risk reduction in progression/death (HR 0.48), median PFS 18.0 vs 8.3 months, ORR 80.2% vs 53.4%.

Outlook Therapeutics — Secured $5.0M registered direct offering from GMS Ventures at $0.5855/share; amended warrants for 15.5M shares to $0.5855 exercise price to fund ONS-5010/LYTENAVA commercialization.
Invivyd — Q1 2026 financial performance — PEMGARDA (pemivibart) — Net product revenue $13.7M, representing 22% growth versus Q1 2025 ($11.3M).
Jasper Therapeutics — Strategic alternatives exploration — briquilimab program — Announced June 1, 2026 to maximize shareholder value; Q1 2026 R&D expense $5.8M, G&A $5.1M.
TG Therapeutics — Conference participation — BRIUMVI (ublituximab-xiiy) — CEO Michael S. Weiss scheduled for Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference fireside chat on June 3, 2026.

SCRI (South Carolina Research Institute) — Outpatient bispecific antibody administration strategy — Teclistamab + Talquetamab with Tocilizumab prophylaxis — Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trial showed 68.9%-71.4% ORR with no grade 3+ toxicity.
